sources.notes: Statement and constraints: fill -1 in b so every length-k window has the same multiset as the corresponding window of a.
Used to confirm the adjacent-window transition condition and modulo-k subsequence decomposition.
Luogu article used to cross-check the pair transition and final first-window count check.
Independent writeup used to confirm decomposition into the first window plus transitions between adjacent windows.

reference_candidates.notes: Requested aggregate Luogu solution page; direct fetch was not used as evidence.

annotation.summary: Given arrays a and partially unknown b, decide whether b can be filled so every corresponding length-k window has equal multisets.
annotation.constraints: Sum n <= 2e5 and values are in [1,n], so the decision must be linear or near-linear.
annotation.core_idea: Compare adjacent windows: moving from [l,l+k-1] to [l+1,l+k] removes a_l and adds a_{l+k}, and b must perform the same multiset change. If a_l != a_{l+k}, then b_l and b_{l+k} are forced to equal the corresponding a values; if a_l == a_{l+k}, then b_l and b_{l+k} only need to be equal. These constraints split into independent chains by index modulo k. Non-constant a-chains force b=a on that chain; constant a-chains allow one shared value. Finally, the first k positions must be fillable as the same multiset as a[1..k], checked by frequency counts of already forced values.
annotation.complexity: O(n) with frequency buckets, or O(n log n) with maps/sets.
tricks: split constraints by index modulo k

solution_variants.name: modulo-chain-constraint-check
solution_variants.summary: For each residue class modulo k, detect whether all a values are equal. Non-constant classes require every known b value to equal a; constant classes require all known b values in the class to agree, then first-window frequencies decide whether remaining unknown classes can be filled.

tags.evidence: The decisive observation is the invariant between adjacent length-k windows: the endpoint removal/insertion delta in b must match the endpoint delta in a.
The endpoint constraints always connect positions i and i+k, so the problem decomposes into independent chains by index modulo k.
tags.description: Split positions by index modulo a fixed step or period so local constraints between i and i+k become independent chains. Use when this decomposition is part of the proof or state reduction, not just loop organization.
tags.created_reason: Existing generic invariant/greedy tags do not capture the reusable modulo-class decomposition that drives this solution.

tags.evidence: The proof of sufficiency constructs or reserves values for unknown b positions, then verifies that the first window can be completed by remaining frequencies.
